name: Prompt brittleness as robustness failure mode
description: Participants described a pattern of unexpected quality degradation following minor changes to prompt templates, and the absence of systematic prompt version control to detect and diagnose these regressions.
key_evidence: Implicit prompt regression: single word changes caused measurable hallucination rate increases (INT-01, INT-03, INT-11)
Prompt version control absent or ad hoc (spreadsheets) — no regression detection capability (INT-05, INT-09, INT-11)
Cross-system prompt instability: prompts tuned for one model degrade on another (INT-05, INT-12)
significance_for_qalis: Motivates RO dimension, particularly RO-1 (perturbation sensitivity) and the CI/CD quality gate pipeline.
